channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stock market today: Wall Street falls after the Federal Reserve hints at just 2 rate cuts for 2025
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stock indexes fell Wednesday after the Federal Reserve hinted it may deliver fewer shots of adrenaline to the economy next year than it had earlier thought. The S&P 500 lost 0.7% to pull a bit further from its all-time high set earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Government funding to run out unless Speaker Mike Johnson can shore up support for bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rushing to prevent a Christmastime government shutdown, House Speaker Mike Johnson scrambled Wednesday to salvage a stopgap measure to keep federal offices running into March despite an onslaught of opposition from hard-right conservatives led by President-elect Donald Trump -ally
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gov. Andy Beshear says Democrats should set partisanship aside, work to meet people's needs
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear said Wednesday that Democrats should be thinking less about partisan politics and more about meeting people's everyday needs as they chart a comeback strategy after last month's crushing election losses.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New charges target ex-Miami congressman for lobbying on behalf of sanctioned Venezuelan tycoon
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A federal grand jury in Washington has returned an indictment against former Congressman David Rivera for failing to register as a foreign agent of a sanctioned Venezuelan media tycoon on whose behalf the Miami Republican allegedly lobbied the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Charlotte Hornets apologize after PS5 taken from 13-year-old during on-court giveaway
item.description: What was supposed to be a festive "skit" during the Charlotte Hornets game against the Philadelphia 76ers on Monday ended up earning the Hornets ‘Grinch’-like jeers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Lankford, KRON4

item.title: Former New Orleans priest, 93, is sentenced to life in prison for raping boy decades ago
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A judge sentenced a 93-year-old former Catholic priest Wednesday to spend the rest of his life behind bars for raping a teenage boy decades ago.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana health officials confirm human case of bird flu: What to know
item.description: "While the current public health risk for the general public is low, people who work with birds, poultry or cows, or have recreational exposure to them, are at higher risk," health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, NewsNation

item.title: Royal Caribbean passenger dies soon after being detained for chaotic incident caught on video
item.description: A Royal Caribbean cruise ship passenger was recently caught on camera acting unruly before being taken down by security. He died an hour later.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Louisiana patient has first severe bird flu case in US: health officials
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Young Americans richer than ever, sense ‘economic fragility’: Report
item.description: While wealth has increased, so has debt. Since 1989, student loan debt grew “nine-fold” due to more people attending college and tuition rates skyrocketing.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart testing body cameras for some in-store employees
item.description: Some in-store Walmart employees are wearing body cameras as part of a pilot program, according to a company spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, already stuck in space, are hit with new delay
item.description: Astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore, the crew of the first manned Boeing Starliner launch in early June, were initially meant to spend only about eight days in space.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service warns of 'smishing,' other holiday scams: What to know
item.description: “While the holiday season offers merriment, joy, and togetherness, fraudsters and their scams can dim the season,” the Postal Service’s federal law enforcement arm warned.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa native named starting QB for Atlanta Falcons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Falcons are turning to rookie Michael Penix Jr. as their starting quarterback and benching veteran Kirk Cousins as they fight for their first playoff berth since 2017. Coach Raheem Morris announced the decision in a statement Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Over 500,000 winter tires recalled due to poor traction in snow
item.description: A tire company that sells winter tires has issued a recall for more than 540,000 of its snow tires, which do not comply with North American standards due to lack of proper traction on snow.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Indiana law shrouds executions in secrecy, prompting new pushes for public oversight
item.description: From public hangings in the town square to lethal injections witnessed by journalists, executions historically have mostly been carried out with at least some public scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A hunter in Virginia died after a bear in a tree was shot, fell on him: authorities
item.description: A Virginia man was killed when a bear was shot out of a tree by one of his hunting partners and fell on him, state wildlife officials announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Community members wrestle with grief in aftermath of Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Community members in Wisconsin continued to wrestle with grief and called for change in the aftermath of a school shooting that killed a teacher and a student and wounded six others.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI and SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US to pay nearly $116M to settle lawsuits over rampant sexual abuse at California women's prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. government will pay nearly $116 million to resolve lawsuits brought by more than 100 women who say they were abused or mistreated at a now-shuttered federal prison in California that was known as the “rape club” because of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect charged with killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO as an act of terrorism
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO has been charged with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors said Tuesday as they worked to bring him to a New York court from a Pennsylvania jail. Luigi Mangione already was charged with murder in the Dec.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Viral study on black kitchen utensils had glaring error, researchers issue correction
item.description: If you were caught up in last month's panic over black kitchen cooking utensils, you now have a reason to breathe easier.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Stock market today: Wall Street trims its stellar gains, Dow slides again
item.description: The Fed is widely expected to announce the third cut of the year to its main interest rate on Wednesday, and officials are also scheduled to unveil projections about where they see rates heading in upcoming years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Things to know about the shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: Police in Madison, Wisconsin, are trying to determine what prompted a 15-year-old student to open fire at a private Christian school. The shooting Monday was just days before the winter break. A fellow student and a teacher were killed. The shooter also died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tech consultant found guilty of second-degree murder in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A San Francisco jury on Tuesday found a tech consultant guilty of second-degree murder in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee, which carries a sentence of 16 years to life, rejecting the defendant's claim that he had acted in self defense.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the 2 killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A student and teacher were killed and six others — five students and a teacher — were injured in Monday's shooting.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect facing murder charges in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings was charged on Tuesday in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager Ben Padua told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, ABC 4

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on a child sex abuse crisis inside a secretive Christian sect
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — For decades, a little-known Christian sect has dealt with its abusive ministers and spiritual elders mostly in secret, shunning legal action in favor of urging victims to forgive the predators in their midst.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Survivors seek a reckoning as FBI investigates child sex abuse in little-known Christian sect
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Nearly every detail about the religious group Lisa Webb’s family belonged to was hidden from the outside world. Its followers met in homes rather than churches. Its leadership structure was hard to discern, its finances opaque. It didn’t even have an official name.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vintage performances by Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers carry Jets past defenseless Jaguars 32-25
item.description: Davante Adams and Aaron Rodgers gave the New York Jets' next general manager and head coach something to ponder with a glimpse into the past — and potentially the future.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey in recent weeks and more have been spotted recently over other states, sparking speculation and concern over who sent them and why.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski And Ken Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas AG sues New York doctor for providing abortion pills across state lines
item.description: Both surgical and medication abortions are banned in Texas, and Friday’s lawsuit is the first legal test to see what happens when state abortion laws conflict with one another.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, WGN 9
